data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T174759-1369eb5a/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T174759-1369eb5a/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T174759-1369eb5a/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T174759-1369eb5a/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T174759-1369eb5a/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7e1fed08">
    <title>evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for both initial implementation cycles and repair cycles with fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
2. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers an initial cycle followed by one review-fixable repair cycle and asserts both trace entries are present in order.
3. TestExecutionTrace_AdapterPreservesCycleFields proves cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go preserves all cycle trace fields in both conversion directions.
4. Existing bead.result/review event consumers remain compatible; if durable field names are retained for compatibility, the test documents those retained names explicitly.
5. No trace writer overwrites or rewrites an older cycle entry for the same result_rev/review_group_id.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecutionTrace|TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, area:evidence, area:review, kind:feature, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, split-from:ddx-9228a484</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="0267b7a3c000951926eae6b789783b7e9495c0ea" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T174759-1369eb5a"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the append-only execution cycle trace end to end and verified it.

- Trace data now lives on the execution report/result shapes in [`execute_bead.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go#L92), with per-cycle route facts and review metadata.
- The candidate-cycle coordinator now appends cumulative trace entries and preserves them across repair cycles in [`candidate_cycle.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle.go#L239).
- Pre-land reviewer dispatch now records review-group ID plus reviewer slot metadata in [`execute_bead_review.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go#L1123).
- `bead.result` event bodies now carry the serialized `cycle_trace` payload in [`execute_bead_loop.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go#L419).
- The legacy `try.Report` adapter preserves the new fields in both directions in [`execute_bead_try_adapter.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go#L9).
- Coverage added for the initial+repair trace flow in [`candidate_cycle_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle_test.go#L719), adapter preservation in [`test_adapters_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/test_adapters_test.go#L170), and event-body compatibility in [`execute_bead_loop_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7e1fed08-20260512T174759-1369eb5a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go#L57).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecutionTrace|TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap" -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Current `HEAD`: `e72a22a76`
